summary: Phase Marketplace v2 + Reputation full end-to-end testing PASSED. Backend: 10/10 pytest passing (4 original + 6 extras covering analytics shape, reputation weights, expertise facets, OpenAlex no-500, complete roles). Frontend: every flow from the review request verified — sidebar nav (Marketplace + Expertise), Marketplace page (9 role chips, filters, sidebar widgets reverse-matches/marketplace-analytics/sidebar-reputation), MatchCard invite modal (toast 'Invitation sent to Lukas Schmidt'), AI rerank (92→87 credits, 22s, top-10 with amber AI SCORE + explanations + 'AI reranked' badge), Expertise CRUD + apply + decide + delete (cross-account elena↔admin), Invitations page (received/sent tabs), Profile reputation badge + invite, responsive at 1366×768 (no h-scroll) and mobile 390×844 (sidebar widgets stack below match cards as confirmed by computed positions sr_top=4280 vs mc_top=685). Zero blocking issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: Reputation cache is 24h but no exposed UI 'force recompute' affordance on the Marketplace sidebar — only the OpenAlex 'Sync ORCID' CTA is present. Consider a small refresh icon on [data-testid='sidebar-reputation'] for users who just earned reviewer/funding credits and want to see updated overall.

After AI rerank the result count chip changes from '15 matches' to '10 matches · AI RERANKED' — semantically correct, but if a user clicks a role chip after rerank, ensure the deterministic 50 are restored and the AI badge cleared (manual test passed; flag for regression coverage).
Expertise create modal accepts skills/areas as comma-separated free-text — no chip preview before submit. Minor UX: tokenize on Enter/comma so users can see what will be stored.
Invitation modal on profile/{id} opens correctly but Cancel button has no data-testid; only Escape works deterministically in tests.

Owner of an expertise request can both close and delete; consider single primary action ('Mark fulfilled') with delete only as destructive secondary, to prevent accidental data loss when applicants exist.
test_credentials: elena.varga@synaptiq.io / demo123 (balance 92 → 87 after one AI rerank), admin@synaptiq.io / admin123
seed_data_creation: Created 1 expertise request 'Need PLS-SEM expert UI test' (id 6a2dc860056e7e9d71113da3) as elena, admin applied with message, elena accepted (status→filled), elena then deleted it — fully cleaned up. 1 marketplace invitation elena→Lukas Schmidt created via UI; remains in system (not cleaned, by design — matches the analytics-counts test expectations).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Marketplace v2 + Reputation phase is green. Pytest suites at /app/backend/tests/test_marketplace_phase.py (4 tests) and /app/backend/tests/test_marketplace_extras.py (6 tests) are regression-ready — run with `BASE_URL=https://idea-to-pub.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_marketplace_phase.py /app/backend/tests/test_marketplace_extras.py -v`. AI rerank consistently completes in ~22s for top-10 with current LLM key; cost is 5 credits/call as expected. OpenAlex sync returned 404 on elena (no resolvable ORCID/name in OpenAlex demo data) — this is the documented acceptable outcome (no 500). For next phases, consider testing: (a) invitation decline path (only accept was exercised here), (b) reverse-match widget click-through to apply, (c) expertise 'matching' tab with skill/area overlap algorithm, (d) reputation /batch endpoint.
rca of the issue: No issues encountered during this iteration. All review-request scenarios behaved exactly as specified.
